Agent / Department HierarchyKnown layers and honest unknowns
HomeSystem StorySystem ScaleOperating ModelLive Dashboard

Hierarchy

The repo has a command hierarchy, not a fake agent census.

The hierarchy is explicit about what is known, what is operationally modeled, and what is still not canonically declared. That keeps the story defensible.

Canonical counts

Exact agent count: UNKNOWN_NOT_CURRENTLY_DECLARED. Exact department count: UNKNOWN_NOT_CURRENTLY_DECLARED.

Level 1 - Product / Command Center Layer

The overall product framing and stakeholder surface.

Level 2 - Readiness Layer Roadmap

MVP-43 through MVP-50 prove readiness layer by layer.

Level 3 - Operational Domains

11 operational domains stand in for formal departments.

Level 4 - Model / Artifact Layer

Manifests, reports, dashboards, and JSON models.

Level 5 - Validator Gate Layer

Flat E2E validators, the wall, and package-specific checks.

Level 6 - Human Review Layer

Stakeholder review, technical review, and executive review.

Level 7 - Future Runtime Activation Layer

Intentionally separate and not started.

Operational domains

What the system currently models

Auth / identity

Defines how access would work in a future runtime.

Request storage

Defines how requests would be persisted.

Audit ledger

Defines how events would be recorded.

Approval gate

Defines how requests would be approved.

Dry-run

Defines how previews happen before any action.

Controlled action queue

Defines ordered handling of requests.

Human-approved execution

Defines the next safety gate, not enabled runtime.

Monitoring / rollback / incident

Defines the observability layer for future runtime.

Stakeholder review

Defines the demo and presentation layer.

Validator / quality gates

Defines machine-checkable proof surfaces.

Safety boundary

Defines the hard stop around runtime activation.

This hierarchy is mirrored in `agent_department_hierarchy.md` and the browser demo keeps the unknown counts explicit.
